summary: Small tooling change in tools/project.py: macOS/darwin was removed from the platform condition that enables wibo in Project.use_wibo(), leaving wibo enabled only on Linux when the tag, architecture, and wrapper conditions are satisfied. Based on the title, this PR moved macOS builds away from wibo and toward the Wine path, but the provided slice only shows the wibo gating change.
what_changed: Changed Project.use_wibo() so sys.platform must equal "linux" instead of allowing either "linux" or "darwin".
Kept the other wibo eligibility checks intact: wibo_tag must be present, platform.machine() must be one of i386/x86_64/aarch64/arm64, and self.wrapper must be None.
Net diff was one line added and one line deleted in tools/project.py.
smart_moves: Implemented the platform behavior change at the central wibo decision point, avoiding scattered macOS-specific conditionals elsewhere.
Preserved the existing architecture and explicit wrapper guards, minimizing risk to Linux wibo behavior while changing only macOS selection.
Used sys.platform string matching consistently with the existing code style.
decomp_lessons: Build-tool platform routing should be controlled in one predicate where possible; this makes future compiler-wrapper changes easier to audit.
When changing host-platform support, keep unrelated guard conditions unchanged so reviewers can see the intended behavioral delta clearly.
For macOS host tooling, be explicit about whether the path should use wibo or Wine; small platform checks can have large effects on build reproducibility.
naming_conventions: Existing method name use_wibo() is a boolean predicate and remains unchanged.
Existing fields wibo_tag and wrapper were kept as the controlling configuration names.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The slice reports two reviews but includes no review-comment excerpt, so reviewer rationale cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mention macOS Wine build support or regressions after removing darwin from use_wibo().
Search tools/project.py history for other uses of sys.platform == "darwin" and wrapper selection.
Check whether documentation or CI configuration was updated elsewhere to describe Wine as the macOS path.
